Container.Name: farm-manager
Container.Overview: Farm Manager — self-hosted PWA for drawing farm fields and sheds on a satellite map and logging timestamped management events (fertilizer, cattle in/out, silage, etc.). Requires the farm-manager-db container to be running first, and both containers must be on the same custom Docker network. Before adding either container run this once in the Unraid terminal: docker network create farm-manager

Config.Name: Web UI port
Config.Description: Port the app listens on. Change the left-hand side if 3000 is already in use on your Unraid host.

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: PostgreSQL connection string. Replace CHANGEME with the same password you set in POSTGRES_PASSWORD on the farm-manager-db container. The hostname 'farm-manager-db' is the name of the database container — leave it as-is unless you renamed that container.

Config.Name: APP_PASSWORD
Config.Description: The password you will use to log in to the app. Pick something you'll remember.

Config.Name: SESSION_SECRET
Config.Description: Random secret used to sign login cookies. Must be at least 32 characters. Generate one in the Unraid terminal with: openssl rand -hex 32

Config.Name: ORIGIN
Config.Description: (Optional but recommended) The public URL of your app, e.g. https://farm.yourdomain.com — required for secure (HTTPS) cookies when accessed via a reverse proxy such as SWAG, Nginx Proxy Manager, or Cloudflare Tunnel. Leave blank for plain HTTP local access.

Config.Name: PUBLIC_APP_NAME
Config.Description: (Optional) Display name shown in the app header and browser tab.
